source.publication: USPS PostalPro — Address Quality Solutions; USPS AMS definitions

chunks.heading: Address Management System (AMS)
chunks.text: The Address Management System (AMS) is the national USPS database of every delivery address with its associated ZIP Code, ZIP+4 code, and city/state name. It serves as the foundation of data for all USPS address correction tools and is the authoritative source used by licensed vendors for address matching.

chunks.heading: DPV — Delivery Point Validation
chunks.text: DPV® (Delivery Point Validation) is a USPS product used to verify that an address is a known delivery point in the USPS database. DPV matching answers whether the secondary (apartment/suite) information is valid at the primary address, beyond the ZIP+4 match.

chunks.heading: LACSLink
chunks.text: LACSLink® (Locatable Address Conversion System) updates rural-style addresses (e.g., Route and Box) to city-style addresses when a 911 emergency system conversion has occurred, and also updates street-style addresses that have been renamed or renumbered.

chunks.heading: SuiteLink
chunks.text: SuiteLink® adds known secondary (suite) information to a business address when that information is missing from the input address and available in the USPS database.

chunks.heading: eLOT — Enhanced Line of Travel
chunks.text: eLOT® (Enhanced Line of Travel) provides mailers with a sequence number for each ZIP+4 that reflects the approximate order in which the delivery point is visited by the carrier, enabling enhanced carrier route presort.

chunks.heading: CASS — Coding Accuracy Support System
chunks.text: CASS™ (Coding Accuracy Support System) is the USPS certification process for address-matching software. CASS certification ensures that a vendor's address matching software meets USPS requirements for ZIP+4, DPV, LACSLink, and SuiteLink matching accuracy; certification is required to qualify mailings for automation postage rates.

chunks.heading: City State File
chunks.text: The City State File is a USPS data product that contains the authoritative list of city and state names (and their 13-character abbreviations) associated with every ZIP Code. Pub 28 references this file for the required last-line city name.
